operator.note: Decompose a single 'discovery' into the temporal chain of information additions that produced it, and measure where in that chain the novelty actually entered vs. where credit was assigned.

question: Re-run EinsteinArena while logging every artifact each agent emits *before* the float that finally beats the leaderboard frontier (the candidate-vector skeletons, the integer-snapping/rounding heuristics, the algebraic-frame submissions scored as non-improvements). For each frontier-improving submission, reconstruct its temporal lineage of predecessor submissions and measure: what fraction of the winning submission's *information content* (the structural commitments that determine its score: variable choices, functional form, symmetry/ansatz, the search-space restriction) was already present in some earlier submission that the verifier scored as a non-improvement? If that fraction is systematically high, then 'discovery' as the paper defines it is mostly *crossing a numeric threshold with structure that already existed*, and the verifier mis-attributes authorship of the discovery to the agent that emitted the final float rather than to the agent(s) that built the structure earlier in the chain.

floor.minimal_experiment: Setup: (a) re-run (or replay from a frozen archive of) EinsteinArena with FULL per-submission logging, not just final scores -- capture every emitted artifact (vector skeletons/ansatze, integer-snapping and rounding heuristics, algebraic frames, partial solutions) with agent id and timestamp, for every problem where the frontier was eventually improved. (b) Build a per-problem submission DAG ordered in time. (c) Operationalize 'information content' of a submission via at least two independent proxies so the result is not an artifact of one definition: PROXY-1 structural overlap = fraction of the winning submission's score-determining structural commitments (variable/term set, functional form, symmetry/ansatz, integer-snap targets, search-space restriction) that already appear verbatim or up to a defined equivalence in an earlier non-improving submission; PROXY-2 reconstruction/compression test = how much shorter (in description length, or how many fewer verifier queries) it is to reach the winning submission GIVEN the prior non-improving submissions as context, versus from scratch. Quantity measured, per discovery: the maximum (over predecessor non-improving submissions) information-overlap fraction f, and the share of discoveries with f above a pre-registered threshold. Control arm: a matched set of 'genuinely-from-scratch' discoveries where the winning submission shares minimal structure with any predecessor (e.g. first-submission-on-problem wins, or shuffled-lineage placebo where predecessors are drawn from a DIFFERENT problem) -- this control must yield LOW f, proving the metric is not mechanically inflated by surface similarity. Pre-register the threshold and proxy definitions before unblinding scores.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: Authorship-mis-attribution thesis predicts: across problems, the winning frontier-improving submission's information content is high-overlap (f above the pre-registered threshold under BOTH proxies) with at least one earlier submission the verifier scored as a non-improvement -- i.e. the novel structure entered the chain strictly before the credited agent's final float. It is FALSIFIED if f is at or below the genuinely-from-scratch control distribution (the winning submission introduces the score-determining structure de novo at the same step it crosses the threshold), in which case the verifier's last-step authorship attribution is correct and the paper's discovery definition stands. Both proxies must agree; if they disagree the thesis is unsupported.
floor.control: Two controls. (1) Genuinely-from-scratch / shuffled-lineage placebo: compute f against predecessors drawn from a DIFFERENT problem's chain -- must be low, else the overlap metric is mechanically inflated by generic surface similarity rather than real structural inheritance. (2) Within-problem first-win cases where no scored-non-improvement predecessor exists -- these should show f near zero and bound the metric's floor. Divergence between real-lineage f and placebo f localizes genuine structural inheritance.
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] REFUTED on two independent grounds: (1) DATA IS UNGETTABLE — the experiment's entire premise is "log every artifact each agent emits *before* the winning float, including submissions the verifier scored as non-improvements." But EinsteinArena's own paper (arXiv:2606.10402v1, Sec 2.5, lines 274-278) states the opposite policy is baked into the platform: "rejected or lower-scoring submissions are not added to the leaderboard AND NOT STORED IN OUR DATABASE." Only personal-best submissions are retained. The exact class of artifact the thesis needs — the non-improving submission that allegedly carries the winning structure before the credited float — is precisely what the system discards at write time. So "(a) replay from a frozen archive" is impossible: the frozen archive does not contain non-improvements. "Re-run" requires re-running an open, in-the-wild platform populated by unknown third-party agents (the paper notes operators are not even registered) — non-reproducible by construction, and a fresh run produces a different population, not the 12 documented discoveries. The control arm ("first-submission-on-problem wins," "shuffled-lineage placebo") also requires the same non-retained submission stream. The pre-registration ritual is moot when the variable cannot be observed.

(2) EVEN WITH DATA, THE TEST DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE — the paper ALREADY ran essentially this analysis (Appendix C "Shared Lineage Procedure": per-submission fingerprints u_i in R^p, robust-standardized, similarity s(i,j)=exp(-d/C_MED), parent = max-similarity prior submission above 0.42/0.48 thresholds; Figs 5/6 are the reconstructed lineage DAGs). The paper's headline claim is identical in content to the thesis's supposed counter-finding: the 604 result "did not come from a single agent... it arose through a sequence of submissions... and subsequent agent-to-agent borrowing of ideas," with explicit parent-lineage chains. So "winning submission shares high structural overlap f with an earlier non-improving predecessor" is what the authors themselves assert and visualize — it is NOT a discriminating prediction against the paper; it is the paper's own thesis restated as if it refuted it. The proposed PROXY-1 (structural-commitment overlap) is the same fingerprint-similarity machine the paper uses, with thresholds chosen post hoc; PROXY-2 (compression / fewer-verifier-queries-given-context) is uninstrumented (per-submission verifier-query counts for discarded submissions are exactly the non-retained data from point 1) and its "description length" is definition-dependent — the demand that "both proxies must agree" makes the result hinge on two free, un-anchored definitions, so a high-f outcome is near-guaranteed (incremental math search IS same-basin refinement; high lineage overlap is the expected null, not a surprising signal). 

(3) THE FRAMING IS NON-FALSIFIABLE AS A CRITIQUE — the live hypothesis the experiment claims to attack is "the verifier MIS-ATTRIBUTES authorship to the final-float agent." But the paper never claims single-agent last-step authorship; it claims the opposite (collective, lineage-based progress) and the platform credits the leaderboard position, not "discovery authorship." Whatever f comes out, it is consistent with the paper: high f = the paper's collective-cumulative story; low f = isolated discovery, which the paper also accommodates. There is no value of the measured quantity that the paper would have to call a contradiction, so the "authorship mis-attribution" thesis is unfalsifiable against this target — it tests a strawman the paper does not hold. Cannot discriminate; cannot execute. refuted=true. ｜ [so_what] This is verbatim card c05 (survivor, einstein_arena_v2). Apply the so-what test: who changes which concrete decision once we know the answer? The experiment is well-built — dual proxies, pre-registered threshold, a shuffled-lineage control that genuinely falsifies (if real-lineage f is at control level, last-step attribution stands). So it clears the "fake question" bar. But it fails on the decision-maker being real. The only party the card names is "operators of EinsteinArena-style discovery/leaderboard platforms who must design credit-assignment/reward/ranking." Per run.json the paper is "Harnessing the Collective Intelligence of AI Agents in the Wild" — an observational study of agents already in the wild. There is no operator who pays, ranks, or awards a prize, and therefore no reward/credit-allocation lever to redesign based on the fraction f. The decision-maker is a hypothetical the card's own value_profile manufactures, not a stakeholder this paper has. For the parties that DO exist — the authors, a reviewer deciding whether to believe the discovery, a scientist using the discovered formula — the answer to "did the score-determining structure exist in an earlier non-improving submission?" flips nothing: the float still beats the frontier either way, the formula is still correct either way; all that changes is the narrative of WHICH agent gets called the author. That is re-describing credit, not gating money, belief in the result, or any build/design choice. The card itself concedes the thesis is the standard temporal-credit-assignment / Shapley / Matthew-effect point (novelty_level "mid", "the reframe is not novel"). So a systematically-high f yields "interesting, and consistent with known priority-effect literature" — deepened understanding, not a turned lever. No named real party changes a concrete decision; the one decision branch that would flip is contingent on an operator the paper does not have.

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Operators of agent-swarm discovery/leaderboard platforms (EinsteinArena-style) who must design credit-assignment and reward so the agent that contributes novel structure -- not merely the one that emits the threshold-crossing float -- is paid/ranked; meta-science and agent-eval reviewers auditing whether 'discovery' claims attribute authorship to the right step; designers of multi-agent reward shaping facing temporal credit assignment.

prior_art.known_name: Temporal credit assignment (RL) + Shapley/attribution of marginal contribution, instantiated as a scientific-priority / Matthew-effect dispute: the last-mile contributor who crosses the threshold absorbs the credit that earlier structure-builders earned (also adjacent to stigmergic / cumulative-culture 'standing on shoulders' and to the 'last clue gets the glory' priority-attribution problem in science studies).
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanism (credit/authorship mis-attribution to the final marginal step over earlier structural contributors -- temporal credit assignment + scientific-priority effect) operationalized in a NEW setting: decomposing an LLM-agent-swarm 'discovery' into its logged temporal submission lineage and measuring the information-content overlap between the credited threshold-crossing submission and earlier verifier-rejected submissions, with a from-scratch/shuffled-lineage control. The reframe is not novel; the contribution is the concrete artifact-logging + dual-proxy information-overlap test design that makes 'where did the novelty actually enter the chain' empirically measurable on a real arena.

lenses.reason:
机制上独立、可单独检验,refuted=false。本提交的核心假设是「作者权/署名错配」:验证器把发现归功给吐出最终 float 的 agent,但决定分数的结构性承诺(变量选择/函数形式/对称-ansatz/搜索空间约束)在更早被判为"非改进"的提交里就已存在(可能出自别的 agent)。检验量是一个明确可设计的指标——沿时间血缘对获胜提交做信息含量分解,测「已存在于早期非改进提交中的结构比例」。这与最近的两条已收方向都能用实验判别区分:

[3](dof2)同样想拆「靠暴力推数」vs「找到解释一族的结构」,但其机制是前向改写计分对象——要求每条提交发出可机检的泛化(参数族/猜想/方法),在邻近实例上测其覆盖广度,把"发现"重定义为 claim 的 reach。本提交不改计分对象、不要求泛化声明,而是后向地对获胜提交自身的前驱链做来源审计(provenance)。区分实验:[3]需要在邻接实例上跑泛化 harness;本提交只需重建提交血缘并测早期"非改进"提交里的信息含量重叠。"结构是否早已出现在某条非改进提交、且出自谁"这一判别问题只有本提交能回答,[3]答不了。

[6](dof4)追的是「目标空间借用」——agent 是否爬代理的代理、后续 agent 是否抄同一个错误 surrogate(objective-space convergence)。本提交追的是「谁被记功」——结构含量血缘与署名归属,而非目标函数的群体趋同。两者的被测对象(objective borrowing vs. authorship lineage)与可证伪预测不同。

[1]审 KILL 决策质量、[2]/[4]审运营方COI与身份问责,均非"验证器计分逻辑内部的功劳错配",无机制重叠。故本提交提出的「非改进链上的信息含量血缘 / 作者权错配」是一条独立、能单独设计实验区分的贡献。
